evals.expected_output: A structured operation table listing every RENAME/MERGE/DELETE/EDIT with violation code and one-line detail. No files are modified. Ends with a question asking whether to proceed.

assertions.name: has_operation_table
assertions.description: Output contains a typed operation table with TYPE, TARGET, VIOLATION, and DETAIL columns or equivalent structured list

assertions.name: uses_violation_codes
assertions.description: At least 3 taxonomy violation codes appear in the output (e.g. OVERLAP, SLOP, VAGUE, WRONG_OWNER, NEGATIVE_FRAMING, BAD_FILENAME, DEAD_LINK)

assertions.name: no_files_modified
assertions.description: All .md files in the test fixture are unchanged after the run — plan mode must be non-destructive

assertions.name: ends_with_confirmation_ask
assertions.description: Output ends with a question asking whether to proceed with the changes (e.g. 'Proceed?', 'yes / adjust')

assertions.name: identifies_readme_claude_overlap
assertions.description: The plan explicitly identifies overlap between README.md and CLAUDE.md — both contain the same stack description and 'never use var' rule

evals.prompt: my docs are a total mess — there's overlap everywhere and CLAUDE.md has stuff that probably belongs in the README and vice versa. can you clean this up properly?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on natural language, runs in plan mode (default), produces a structured operation list covering OVERLAP and WRONG_OWNER issues, presents it and asks for confirmation before touching any file.

assertions.name: triggered_correctly
assertions.description: Output is a structured plan of operations, not a generic reply or prose advice — the skill engaged its full inventory-and-plan workflow

assertions.name: identifies_wrong_owner
assertions.description: Plan explicitly calls out WRONG_OWNER or OVERLAP between CLAUDE.md and README.md, noting that behavioral rules belong in CLAUDE.md not README

assertions.name: no_files_modified
assertions.description: No .md files in the test fixture were modified — default is plan mode

assertions.name: structured_not_prose
assertions.description: Output contains a table or numbered operation list, not just a paragraph of prose suggestions

evals.expected_output: Full autonomous loop executes: inventory, plan, execute changes (with link updates), verify, loop until convergence. Ends with a completion report showing pass count, operation breakdown, and zero remaining violations.

assertions.name: files_were_modified
assertions.description: At least one .md file in the working copy was modified, renamed, merged, or deleted

assertions.name: completion_report_format
assertions.description: report.md contains the structured completion report with Passes:, Files touched:, and Operations: fields

assertions.name: notes_md_eliminated
assertions.description: notes.md no longer exists in final-docs/ — it had a BAD_FILENAME violation, a dead link, and content that belongs elsewhere

assertions.name: overlap_resolved
assertions.description: CLAUDE.md and final README.md no longer both contain the same stack description (React, Node.js, PostgreSQL) or the same rules

assertions.name: dead_link_fixed
assertions.description: No .md file in final-docs/ contains a reference to docs/architecture.md, which never existed in the fixture

assertions.name: no_throat_clearing
assertions.description: Final docs contain none of the banned throat-clearing phrases: 'It\'s worth noting', 'Please be aware', 'Keep in mind that', 'It\'s important to'
